name: Join-String

options.name: -DoubleQuote
options.description: Wraps the string value of each pipeline object in double-quotes.

options.name: -FormatString
options.description: Specifies a format string that specifies how each pipeline object should be formatted before joining them. Use the `{0}` placeholder to represent the current object. If you need to keep the curly braces (`{}`) in the formatted string, you can escape them by doubling the curly braces (`{{` and `}}`).

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: Specifies the text to be joined. Enter a variable that contains the text, or type a command or expression that gets the objects to join into strings.

name: -OutputPrefix
-op
options.description: Text that's inserted before the output string. The string can contain special characters such as carriage return (`` `r ``), newline (`` `n ``), and tab (`` `t ``).

name: -OutputSuffix
-os
options.description: Text that's appended to the output string. The string can contain special characters such as carriage return (`` `r ``), newline (`` `n ``), and tab (`` `t ``).

options.name: -Property
options.description: The name of a property, or a property expression, to be converted to text.

options.name: -Separator
options.description: Text or characters such as a comma or semicolon that's inserted between the text for each pipeline object.

options.name: -SingleQuote
options.description: Wraps the string value of each pipeline object in single quotes.

options.name: -UseCulture
options.description: Uses the list separator for the current culture as the item delimiter. To find the list separator for a culture, use the following command: `(Get-Culture).TextInfo.ListSeparator`.

description: Combines objects from the pipeline into a single string.
